title: Cadetia trigonocarpa
taxonomy: Cadetia trigonocarpa is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Cadetia, which comprises several species of epiphytic orchids. The exact classification of Cadetia trigonocarpa is within the subtribe Gynoponthicinae, which is part of the tribe Cranichideae. This species is further grouped into the tribe Cranichideae, along with other species of orchids. The classification of Cadetia trigonocarpa reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the orchid family.

intro: Cadetia trigonocarpa, also known as the "cadetia" or "bird's tongue orchid," is a rare and exotic plant species belonging to the orchid family (Orchidaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its potential health benefits such as antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Cadetia trigonocarpa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cadetia trigonocarpa is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to treat various ailments, with the most common preparations being a decoction made with the plant's leaves and roots.
